CommandGroupShorthandFlags.brief: A collection of constexpr bit flags representing shorthand identifiers for various command groups used in permission management and command categorization.
CommandGroupShorthandFlags.details: The CommandGroupShorthandFlags group defines a set of constexpr constants, each representing a specific command group or attribute within the system. These flags are used to categorize entities such as players, mobile characters, clans, avatars, heroes, deputies, leaders, generation-related commands, quests, build permissions, coding functionalities, and security attributes. By utilizing bit flags, the system can perform efficient permission checks, command routing, and role identification, supporting modular and scalable management of command permissions across different system components. These constants are integral to organizing command logic, enforcing security, and enabling quick recognition of entity roles and command attributes during runtime.
CommandGroupShorthandFlags.notes: All flags are designed as bit masks, allowing multiple attributes or group memberships to be combined efficiently using bitwise operations. This approach supports scalable permission management and simplifies the process of checking for multiple roles or attributes simultaneously.
CommandGroupShorthandFlags.rationale: This group exists to provide a standardized and efficient way to categorize and identify command groups and attributes within the system. By defining these flags as constants, the codebase ensures consistency in permission handling, facilitates quick lookups, and supports the modular organization of command-related logic, ultimately enhancing system security, maintainability, and performance.
CommandGroupShorthandFlags.name: CommandGroupShorthandFlags

GP.brief: A bit flag representing the 'Group Player' command group shorthand used for identifying player-related entities.
GP.details: The constant `CommandGroupShorthandFlags::GP` is initialized with the `Flags::Bit` value `GROUP_PLAYER`, which designates entities associated with the player group in command shorthand contexts. It enables efficient identification and manipulation of player-specific entities through bitwise operations, supporting quick checks and categorization within command processing routines. This flag is part of a broader set of shorthand flags used to streamline entity grouping and command targeting mechanisms in the system.
GP.notes: This flag is stored as a bit within a bitmask, allowing multiple flags to be combined and checked efficiently. Its use aligns with the system's design to manage entity attributes and permissions compactly and perform rapid bitwise operations.
GP.rationale: The constant exists to facilitate quick and efficient recognition of entities belonging to the player group during command processing and gameplay interactions, supporting features like grouping, permissions, and specialized handling of player-related entities.
GP.name: GP

GM.brief: A bit flag indicating the 'Mobile' command group shorthand used to categorize commands for mobile (NPC) characters.
GM.details: The constant GM is a specific bit flag derived from the GROUP_MOBILE flag within the CommandGroupShorthandFlags namespace. It serves to identify command groups associated with mobile (NPC) characters, enabling efficient categorization and filtering through bitwise operations. This flag is initialized with the value of the constant GROUP_MOBILE and is often combined with other flags to define or restrict command group behaviors related to mobile entities within the system. Its use supports clear separation of command functionalities and permissions for different character types.
GM.notes: This flag is stored as a single bit within a bitmask, allowing for compact storage and rapid checks using bitwise operations. It is part of a broader set of flags used to manage command group attributes efficiently.
GM.rationale: The GM constant exists to facilitate the identification and management of command groups associated with mobile characters, ensuring that commands intended for NPCs are properly categorized and restricted. This supports correct game logic, permissions, and command filtering related to mobile entities.
GM.name: GM

GC.brief: A bit flag that indicates the 'Group Clan' command group shorthand.
GC.details: The constant `CommandGroupShorthandFlags::GC` is initialized with the `Flags::Bit` value `GROUP_CLAN`, representing the shorthand for clan-related command groups within the system. It is used to categorize and identify commands associated with clans, enabling efficient filtering, permission checks, and command handling based on clan membership or affiliation. This flag is part of a broader set of bit flags that support compact storage and rapid bitwise operations for managing command groups and permissions across various system components.
GC.notes: This flag is stored as a bit within a bitmask, allowing multiple command group shorthands to be combined and checked efficiently using bitwise operations.
GC.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear and efficient way to mark commands related to clans, facilitating consistent permission management and command categorization within the application.
GC.name: GC

GAV.brief: A bit flag indicating membership in the 'avatar' command group for high-level character status commands.
GAV.details: The constant GAV is a specific bit flag within the CommandGroupShorthandFlags namespace, initialized to the value of GROUP_AVATAR from CommandGroupFlags. It serves to identify or manipulate command groups associated with avatar or high-level character status functionalities. By representing this attribute as a bit flag, it allows for efficient bitwise operations—such as checks, sets, or clears—across the system, supporting organized permission management and command handling related to character avatars. This setup enables scalable and performant management of character permissions and behaviors within the application's command infrastructure.
GAV.notes: This constant is stored as a bit flag within a compact bitfield, allowing multiple command group flags to be combined and checked efficiently using bitwise operations.
GAV.rationale: The GAV constant exists to facilitate quick and reliable management of avatar-related command permissions, ensuring that high-level character status commands can be enabled, disabled, or verified efficiently within the game's permission system.
GAV.name: GAV

GH.brief: A bit flag indicating membership in the 'hero' command group within the system.
GH.details: The constant GH is a predefined bit flag used to identify entities that belong to the 'hero' command group. It is initialized with the value of CommandGroupFlags::GROUP_HERO, enabling efficient bitwise operations for checking or setting hero-related attributes. This flag is part of a collection of flags managed through the Flags::Bit enumeration, which allows for compact storage and rapid evaluation of group memberships and attributes across the application. Its use supports features and mechanics that depend on distinguishing hero characters from others.
GH.notes: This constant is stored as a bit flag within a bitmask, facilitating quick bitwise checks and modifications. It is designed to be combined with other flags to represent multiple group memberships or attributes simultaneously.
GH.rationale: GH exists to provide a clear and efficient means of identifying hero characters within the system, supporting gameplay mechanics, permissions, and feature toggling that depend on hero status. Using a bit flag allows for scalable and performant management of multiple attributes and group memberships.
GH.name: GH

GD.brief: A bit flag indicating the 'GD' shorthand for deputy status within command groups.
GD.details: The constant `CommandGroupShorthandFlags::GD` is a compile-time constant initialized to the `Flags::Bit::GROUP_DEPUTY` flag. It represents the shorthand notation 'GD' used to identify deputy or head members within a command group, enabling efficient role recognition and management through bitwise operations. This flag is part of a collection of shorthand flags that encode specific roles or attributes, supporting quick status checks and role-based logic in group-related functionalities across the system.
GD.notes: This flag is stored as a specific bit within a bitmask, allowing multiple role attributes to be combined and checked efficiently using bitwise operations.
GD.rationale: The constant exists to provide a standardized, efficient way to mark and identify deputy members within command groups, supporting role-based permissions and hierarchical management in the application.
GD.name: GD

GL.brief: A constant representing the 'Group Leader' flag within command group shorthand flags.
GL.details: The `CommandGroupShorthandFlags::GL` constant is a bit flag used to denote the 'Group Leader' status within command group shorthand representations. It is initialized with the value of `Flags::Bit::GROUP_LEADER`, enabling it to be combined with other flags through bitwise operations for efficient status management. This constant plays a key role in quickly identifying entities with leadership or imp status within command groups, thereby supporting role-based permissions, authority checks, and hierarchical control mechanisms across the system.
GL.notes: This constant is stored as a specific bit within a bitfield, allowing multiple flags to be efficiently combined and checked using bitwise operations.
GL.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, efficient way to represent and verify leadership roles within command groups, facilitating scalable permission management and role-based behavior in the application.
GL.name: GL

GWG.brief: A bit flag indicating the 'generation' command group within the command group shorthand flags.
GWG.details: The constant GWG is a specific bit flag used to represent the 'generation' command group in the system's shorthand flags. It is initialized with the value of CommandGroupFlags::GROUP_GEN, which categorizes commands related to generation features. As part of the Flags::Bit enumeration, GWG enables efficient bitwise operations for managing command group attributes, facilitating quick checks and filtering of commands associated with generation functionalities within the application's command processing framework. This setup supports modular and scalable command management by associating related commands with a dedicated flag.
GWG.notes: GWG is stored as a bit within a bitmask, allowing multiple command group flags to be combined efficiently using bitwise operations.
GWG.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear and efficient way to identify and handle commands related to the 'generation' functionality, supporting modular command categorization and permission checks within the system.
GWG.name: GWG

GWQ.brief: A bit flag that indicates the 'quest' command group within the command categorization system.
GWQ.details: The constant GWQ is initialized with the value of CommandGroupFlags::GROUP_QUEST, representing the 'quest' command group. It functions as a specific bit within a set of flags used to categorize commands related to quests, enabling efficient grouping and filtering through bitwise operations. This flag is utilized throughout the application to identify, manage, and process quest-related commands, supporting modular command handling and permission checks. Its design aligns with the system's approach of using individual bits to represent different command groups for scalable and maintainable command management.
GWQ.notes: This flag is stored as a bit within a Flags::Bit enumeration, allowing multiple command groups to be combined and checked efficiently using bitwise operations.
GWQ.rationale: The GWQ constant exists to provide a clear and efficient way to identify quest-related command groups, facilitating modular command processing and permission management within the system.
GWQ.name: GWQ

GWB.brief: A bit flag indicating the 'build' permission within command group shorthand flags.
GWB.details: The constant `CommandGroupShorthandFlags::GWB` is initialized to the `GROUP_BUILD` flag, which is a specific bit within the `Flags::Bit` enumeration. It is used to represent the 'build' permission in command group configurations, enabling efficient permission checks through bitwise operations. This flag can be combined with other shorthand flags to define the capabilities of a command group, facilitating flexible and compact permission management across the system.
GWB.notes: This flag is stored as a single bit within a bitfield, allowing multiple permissions to be combined and checked efficiently using bitwise operators.
GWB.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear and efficient way to represent and verify the 'build' permission within command group configurations, supporting secure and organized permission management in the application.
GWB.name: GWB

GWC.brief: A bit flag indicating the 'code' command group within the command grouping system.
GWC.details: The constant GWC is a specific bit flag that represents the 'code' command group, facilitating the categorization of commands related to coding or programming functionalities. It is initialized with the value of CommandGroupFlags::GROUP_CODE, ensuring consistency and clarity in command classification. As part of the Flags::Bit enumeration, GWC enables efficient bitwise operations for managing command groups, allowing multiple flags to be combined and checked rapidly. This setup supports organized command processing and permission management across the application, especially when handling complex command sets that require flexible grouping.
GWC.notes: GWC is stored as a bit flag within a compact bitfield, enabling efficient storage and quick bitwise checks during command processing.
GWC.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear and efficient way to identify and manage commands related to coding functionalities, supporting organized command categorization and permission control within the system.
GWC.name: GWC

GWS.brief: A bit flag representing the 'secure' attribute for command groups within the system.
GWS.details: The constant GWS is a specific bit flag within the CommandGroupShorthandFlags namespace, initialized with the value of CommandGroupFlags::GROUP_SECURE. It is used to denote command groups that are designated as secure or restricted, enabling the system to identify and enforce security policies efficiently. This flag can be combined with other flags using bitwise operations to manage multiple command group attributes collectively, supporting secure command execution and access control mechanisms across the application.
GWS.notes: This flag is stored as a bit within a bitmask, allowing for efficient combination and checking of multiple command group attributes. Its use alongside other shorthand flags facilitates scalable attribute management.
GWS.rationale: The GWS constant exists to provide a clear and consistent way to mark command groups as secure, supporting the application's security model by enabling quick identification and enforcement of security restrictions on command groups.
GWS.name: GWS
